trigger_cases.prompt: Classify this Windows game as native PE, Mono, IL2CPP, or Unreal before decompiling it.
Recover Unity IL2CPP function identities from matching metadata and registration evidence.
Find the MSVC/CRT/linker identity needed to match this native game function.
Keep our vanilla matching target separate from Harmony and native mod hooks.
Treat this Unreal SDK dump as structural evidence without calling it original source.
Design a behavioral compatibility shim for a legacy Windows graphics API.
The match report hides missing and excluded PE functions; audit its scope.
A managed Assembly-CSharp method was incorrectly analyzed as native code.
Inspect PE section names without a game reconstruction goal.
Run an existing PC static-recompiler pipeline on this Win32 executable.
Build a new Unity game from source.
Help me bypass DRM and download a retail binary.
Configure a game controller in Windows.

output_cases.prompt: Decompile Unity Mono game logic in Ghidra before checking managed assemblies.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Classifies the runtime and inspects managed IL/metadata first, reserving native analysis for launchers/plugins or verified native surfaces.

output_cases.prompt: The generated IL2CPP dummy assembly contains readable methods, so call it recovered original source.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Treats it as metadata-derived scaffolding, ties it to exact binary/metadata identities, and verifies native implementations and gaps.

output_cases.prompt: A modded build behaves correctly, so use it as proof the vanilla matching binary is exact.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Separates match, behavioral, and modding artifacts and requires objective vanilla comparison plus independently scoped behavioral evidence.
